Pricing.title: One payment. No subscription.
Pricing.subtitle: Lemon Squeezy as Merchant of Record — VAT for DE/EU/UK/USA handled automatically.
Pricing.comingSoon: Lemon Squeezy listing coming — sign up for the launch email:

items.a: Access to the private GitHub repo. You clone it, fill in .env values (Supabase, Stripe, Resend), deploy to Vercel. Time to first live version: ~2 hours.

items.q: Do I need a lawyer for the compliance components?
items.a: Saaslyde ships the templates a lawyer would otherwise build manually. For standard use cases (B2C SaaS, B2B tools, newsletters) you do NOT need a lawyer. For special cases (subprocessors outside the EU, health data, kids' apps) get the contract reviewed — typically a one-time €100-300 spend.

items.q: Does this only work in Germany?
items.a: No. GDPR applies EU-wide. TDDDG (cookie law) is Germany's transposition of the EU ePrivacy directive and almost identical to France's LCEN, Austria's DSG, etc. Saaslyde is DE/EN bilingual.

items.q: What if the code is outdated in 6 months?
items.a: Starter = 1 year of updates included. Pro = lifetime. Stacks used (Next.js, Supabase, Stripe, Tailwind) are LTS-relevant — the boilerplate typically stays stable 12-24 months without major refactors.

items.q: Can I resell the Saaslyde boilerplate itself?
items.a: No. You get a license to build UNLIMITED of your OWN SaaS products on top (Pro) or ONE product (Starter). Reselling or redistributing the boilerplate code itself is not allowed.
30-day money-back guarantee, no questions asked. Through Lemon Squeezy.

Footer.tagline: GDPR-compliant SaaS boilerplate for indie hackers in the EU.
